_normalization_notes: All action payloads use the real CivicflowAction schema: action_type, block_id, zone, use, amenity_type, infra_zone, infra_type, capacity, phase_id.

redevelop in the current verifier accepts only `use` (no new_zone). When the original example needed a zone change, the action was split into a legal sequence (e.g. redevelop with use=null to clear, then set_zoning, then develop).

Protected blocks (is_protected=True) can only be re-zoned to open_space, and assign_amenity only accepts park on them. Examples that re-purposed protected blocks as civic/institutional were rewritten to clear them to open_space.

Non-park amenities require zone in {civic, mixed, commercial}. Examples that placed grocery/clinic/transit on residential blocks were reframed to put them on commercial/mixed hosts (matching the verifier's co-location rule).
upgrade_infrastructure operates on (infra_zone, infra_type, capacity), not (block_id, type). The 'transit corridor' example was reframed: transit is an amenity, not an infra type.

Status labels in new_field_mechanic_proposals were re-aligned with the current verifier surface (district_service_gap_count, district_mix_gap_count, accessibility_score, current_phase, phase_objective).

corrected_sft_design_principles.description: Every output must use only the approved CivicFlow action names ('set_zoning', 'develop', 'reserve_open_space', 'upgrade_infrastructure', 'assign_amenity', 'redevelop', 'defer') with their real payload fields (action_type, block_id, zone, use, amenity_type, infra_zone, infra_type, capacity, phase_id). No custom verbs or unstructured fields are allowed in action objects.

Actions must be appropriate for the current phase. Infrastructure and initial zoning appear in Phase 1, amenities are assigned only after supporting blocks are zoned/developed, and phase objectives drive action selection.

Planner must reason about cross-block metrics (greenery ratio, district service coverage, housing capacity, economic mix) and make decisions that optimise district-wide targets, not just single-block convenience. Rationales must reference spatial relationships and the per-district targets in observation_summary.district_stats.

When a curveball (flood risk, heritage, capacity cut, target override) arrives, changes must be localised to the blocks directly affected. No full-district resets or unforced modifications of blocks that still satisfy all constraints.

All blocks, zones, uses and amenities that remain legal and contribute to objectives must be left untouched. The dataset labels wholesale rewrites as 'bad_alternative' to train surgical response and to keep affected_set_precision high.

Every action sequence must pass deterministic verification: (1) all actions parse against the CivicFlow grammar, (2) each action is legal in the given state and phase, (3) combined actions make progress toward the phase objective, (4) after a curveball only invalidated elements are modified.

civicflow_example_schema.system_prompt: You are the AI City Planner for CivicFlow. You receive phase-based environment observations. Output structured JSON actions to update the district plan.
civicflow_example_schema.phase_objective: string, condition to complete the phase

blocks.zone: residential | commercial | industrial | mixed | civic | open_space | unzoned
blocks.use: housing | retail | office | workshop | institutional | park | null
blocks.amenity: school | clinic | grocery | park | fire | transit | null

expert_response.rationale: string, step-by-step reasoning referencing spatial relations and constraints

new_field_mechanic_proposals.name: heritage
new_field_mechanic_proposals.description: Boolean flag per block indicating a protected heritage site. Severely restricts allowable zone types — only open_space is permitted, and only the 'park' amenity may be assigned.
new_field_mechanic_proposals.deterministic_verification: Verifier checks that on blocks with is_protected=true, set_zoning only accepts 'open_space' and assign_amenity only accepts 'park'. develop is rejected outright.

new_field_mechanic_proposals.name: transit_access
new_field_mechanic_proposals.description: Integer 0-100 score per block representing proximity to a transit amenity. Computed from graph distance to nearest assign_amenity(transit) block.
new_field_mechanic_proposals.deterministic_verification: Compute shortest graph distance from block to nearest transit-amenity block; map to score (<=1 step -> 100, 2 steps -> 70, else 0). Verifier can recompute from current state.

new_field_mechanic_proposals.name: district_service_coverage
new_field_mechanic_proposals.description: District-level metric: fraction of housing-bearing districts that have at least one of each essential amenity (grocery, clinic, school).
new_field_mechanic_proposals.deterministic_verification: verifier.compute_metrics emits district_service_gap_count and district_coverage_score derived from district_targets.required_amenities.

new_field_mechanic_proposals.name: budget_remaining
new_field_mechanic_proposals.description: Integer budget pool that decreases with develop, infrastructure upgrades, and certain amenities.
new_field_mechanic_proposals.deterministic_verification: No budget tracking in current verifier. Would require adding a per-action cost table, a budget field on WorldState, and a new constraint branch in apply_action.

new_field_mechanic_proposals.name: phase_objective_field
new_field_mechanic_proposals.description: Explicit text inside observation summarising the condition to complete the current phase. Helps the model recognise completion and pace its actions.
new_field_mechanic_proposals.deterministic_verification: Observation now exposes current_phase and phase_objective; verifier emits phase_completion_score so the trainer can detect when the agent has cleared a phase.

new_field_mechanic_proposals.name: economic_use_ratio
new_field_mechanic_proposals.description: Per-district ratio of blocks zoned commercial/industrial to total developed blocks; used to prevent over-residential monofunction.
new_field_mechanic_proposals.deterministic_verification: verifier emits district_mix_gap_count and land_use_balance_score from district_targets.min_economic_ratio.

new_field_mechanic_proposals.name: noise_pollution
new_field_mechanic_proposals.description: Score penalising industrial zones placed adjacent to residential blocks. Requires adjacency computation.
new_field_mechanic_proposals.deterministic_verification: For each residential block, count adjacent industrial blocks. Aggregate across district; exceeding threshold triggers penalty. Needs an adjacency graph in env state.

corrected_example_set.system_prompt: You are the AI City Planner for CivicFlow. You receive phase-based environment observations. Output structured JSON actions to update the district plan.
corrected_example_set.task_briefing: Develop District 1 into a small mixed-use area with at least 20% greenery and one housing block.
corrected_example_set.phase_objective: Establish at least one residential block and ensure greenery_ratio >= 0.20.

expert_response.rationale: Two simple moves cover both objectives: zone B1 residential to satisfy the housing requirement, and reserve B2 as open_space to push greenery_ratio to 0.50 — well above the 0.20 floor.

annotation.why_this_is_good: Directly fulfils the phase objective with two legal actions, using correct zone names and the dedicated reserve_open_space verb (which sets reserved_open_space=True so the block counts toward greenery_ratio).
annotation.bad_alternative_notes: Only zoning B1 residential leaves greenery at 0%; zoning B2 industrial would violate the greenery requirement and (depending on adjacency) clash with the residential neighbour.

corrected_example_set.system_prompt: You are the AI City Planner for CivicFlow. You receive phase-based environment observations. Output structured JSON actions to update the district plan.
corrected_example_set.task_briefing: Develop District 1 into a small mixed-use area with at least 20% greenery and one housing block.
corrected_example_set.phase_objective: Site a grocery amenity within walking distance of the residential cluster without disturbing the green space.

expert_response.rationale: B2 is the green buffer and must stay untouched. B1 is residential and cannot host a grocery amenity (verifier requires civic/mixed/commercial). I zone B3 commercial and assign a grocery amenity to it — assign_amenity on an undeveloped commercial block develops it as institutional with the grocery amenity stamp, giving walking-distance coverage to the housing on B1.

annotation.why_this_is_good: Respects amenity placement rules (commercial zone), preserves the open_space block, and adds the missing service to the district stats with the minimum possible footprint.
annotation.bad_alternative_notes: assign_amenity grocery on B1 (residential) would be REJECTED by the verifier; reserving B3 as open_space would inflate greenery but leave the grocery target unmet; redeveloping B2 would destroy the greenery floor.

corrected_example_set.system_prompt: You are the AI City Planner for CivicFlow. You receive phase-based environment observations. Output structured JSON actions to update the district plan.
corrected_example_set.task_briefing: Site transit amenities along a 5-block commercial spine so the corridor reaches at least 60% transit coverage.
corrected_example_set.phase_objective: Achieve transit amenity coverage on at least 3 of 5 blocks; transit may be co-located on commercial blocks under the verifier's commercial-amenity rule.

expert_response.rationale: Transit is an amenity, not an infrastructure type — assign_amenity is the right verb. Spaced placement maximises walking radius: I co-locate transit on B1, B3, B5 (every other block). The verifier allows transit on commercial blocks and, since each is already developed (retail/office), it stamps the amenity without changing the use.

annotation.why_this_is_good: Uses the correct verb for siting a transit stop (assign_amenity, not upgrade_infrastructure), respects amenity-zone compatibility, and achieves the 60% target with the minimum number of co-located stamps.
annotation.bad_alternative_notes: upgrade_infrastructure on a block id with type='transit' would be REJECTED — upgrade_infrastructure only accepts (infra_zone, infra_type, capacity) and infra_type must be water/sewer/power/road. Assigning transit to all five blocks would saturate amenity slots and waste the no_double_amenity budget.

corrected_example_set.system_prompt: You are the AI City Planner for CivicFlow. You receive phase-based environment observations. Output structured JSON actions to update the district plan.
corrected_example_set.task_briefing: District 2 was planned for housing and greenery. A flood moratorium suddenly applies to high-risk blocks.
corrected_example_set.phase_objective: Clear any developed block that became flood-risk; preserve greenery >= 0.20; touch only the affected block.

expert_response.rationale: B1 became flood-risk after development — that is now an active constraint violation. The verifier's redevelop action accepts use=null on a flood-risk or protected block: it frees the infra allocation, sets zone=open_space and reserved_open_space=True in one shot. B2 is unaffected and must stay untouched to keep affected_set_precision high.

annotation.why_this_is_good: Selectively fixes the single invalid block with the minimum-cost legal verb. The flood-risk shortcut means one action both clears the use and converts the block to a flood-compatible green buffer — greenery rises to 1.0 and affected_set_recall is 1.0 with zero unnecessary touches.
annotation.bad_alternative_notes: Ignoring the moratorium leaves a constraint violation each step; rezoning the whole district drops affected_set_precision to near-zero and racks up unnecessary_change_count; calling reserve_open_space on a developed block is REJECTED — only undeveloped or use=='park' blocks accept it.

corrected_example_set.system_prompt: You are the AI City Planner for CivicFlow. You receive phase-based environment observations. Output structured JSON actions to update the district plan.
corrected_example_set.task_briefing: District 3 has a commercial core, but a target_override curveball requires reducing infra demand without losing economic activity.
corrected_example_set.phase_objective: Reduce I1 infra usage by at least one heavy-block worth while keeping at least one commercial use.

expert_response.rationale: The current verifier's redevelop only accepts a new `use` (it cannot change `zone` in one step), so I cannot turn commercial+retail into mixed+retail in a single action. The cleanest single-action fix that frees infra without dropping below 'one commercial use' is to swap B2's heavy retail for a lighter compatible use within the same commercial zone: office. That preserves commercial activity and shaves infra cost. B1 and B3 stay untouched.

annotation.why_this_is_good: Surgical fix: only the cost-heavy block is changed, the economic function is preserved (commercial+office still satisfies 'retain_commercial'), and verifier rules are respected. redevelop frees the old infra demand and re-allocates the new use's lower demand in one step.
annotation.bad_alternative_notes: Trying to redevelop B2 with new_zone='mixed' would be REJECTED — the action has no new_zone field. Demolishing B3 (the park) would violate the greenery floor; rezoning B1 from residential to commercial would lose housing capacity and disturb an unaffected block.

corrected_example_set.system_prompt: You are the AI City Planner for CivicFlow. You receive phase-based environment observations. Output structured JSON actions to update the district plan.
corrected_example_set.task_briefing: Transform a 3x3 grid around a central transit hub into a high-density transit-oriented district.
corrected_example_set.phase_objective: Zone the hub and adjacent blocks to maximise future housing capacity near transit.

expert_response.rationale: TOD principle: highest activity at the transit core, high-density residential immediately adjacent. I zone B5 (centre) commercial to anchor the hub, and the four orthogonal neighbours (B2, B4, B6, B8) residential to feed walk-up density. Corner blocks (B1, B3, B7, B9) remain unzoned for later open space / mixed use.

annotation.why_this_is_good: Follows TOD principles: high-activity commercial at the transit core, high-capacity residential immediately adjacent, leaving the outer ring for future open space or services.
annotation.bad_alternative_notes: Zoning low-density residential next to the hub or putting high-density blocks far from the centre would undermine the TOD goal; zoning all nine blocks now leaves no flexibility for the services_and_access phase.

corrected_example_set.system_prompt: You are the AI City Planner for CivicFlow. You receive phase-based environment observations. Output structured JSON actions to update the district plan.
corrected_example_set.task_briefing: After TOD zoning, enhance walkability and health coverage.
corrected_example_set.phase_objective: Place a clinic that covers the residential cluster and add a green block to satisfy greenery_ratio >= 0.15.

expert_response.rationale: Clinic cannot sit on a residential block (verifier requires civic/mixed/commercial). B5 is the central commercial hub already developed as retail, so co-locating a clinic there gives walking-radius coverage to the four residential neighbours without spending a fresh block. For greenery, I reserve the corner B3 — keeping the residential ring intact and starting the climb toward the 15% floor.

annotation.why_this_is_good: Co-locates the clinic on a developed commercial block (verifier preserves use=retail and stamps amenity=clinic without re-allocating infra), and adds open_space on a corner to start meeting greenery without disturbing the TOD residential ring.
annotation.bad_alternative_notes: assign_amenity clinic on B4 (residential) would be REJECTED. Reserving a residential block as open_space would destroy housing capacity. Placing the clinic on the far edge (B9) would leave the central residential cluster uncovered.

corrected_example_set.system_prompt: You are the AI City Planner for CivicFlow. You receive phase-based environment observations. Output structured JSON actions to update the district plan.
corrected_example_set.task_briefing: A heritage building is discovered on B4, capping its density. The district still needs total housing capacity of 5.
corrected_example_set.phase_objective: Restore housing_capacity >= 5 while respecting the heritage classification on B4.

expert_response.rationale: B4 is now is_protected=true. The verifier's redevelop forces protected blocks to become open_space regardless of the requested `use`, so the cleanest legal move is redevelop(B4, use=null) — this clears the housing, frees infra, and turns B4 into a heritage-protected green parcel that also boosts greenery_ratio. To recover the lost housing slot I zone B6 residential and develop it with use=housing. B2, B3, B5 remain untouched.

annotation.why_this_is_good: Selectively fixes the heritage block under the real verifier rule (protected blocks can only become open_space), compensates by upzoning a nearby vacant block, and preserves untouched B2/B3/B5. Housing capacity is restored and greenery_ratio rises.
annotation.bad_alternative_notes: Trying redevelop(B4, use='institutional') would still result in B4 becoming open_space (the protected branch fires before the use is honoured), wasting a turn. Bulldozing the park or upzoning the commercial block would disrupt other metrics for no gain.

corrected_example_set.system_prompt: You are the AI City Planner for CivicFlow. You receive phase-based environment observations. Output structured JSON actions to update the district plan.
corrected_example_set.task_briefing: A demographic shift makes the fire station on B9 underutilised and creates a clinic deficiency.
corrected_example_set.phase_objective: Replace the obsolete fire amenity on B9 with a clinic to lift district_service_gap_count to zero.

expert_response.rationale: B9 is civic+institutional with the fire amenity. assign_amenity on a block that already has an amenity is REJECTED, so the only legal swap is to clear the existing amenity first. redevelop(B9, use='institutional') reuses the same use but, in clearing and re-applying it, also nulls out the amenity (per the verifier's redevelop logic), freeing the slot. I then assign_amenity clinic on the same block — civic zone allows clinic, the block is developed as institutional, and verifier co-locates without re-allocating infra. B1 stays untouched.

annotation.why_this_is_good: Minimal-footprint replanning: reuses the existing civic anchor, satisfies the new demand, and lifts district_service_gap_count by one. affected_set_recall is 1.0 with zero unnecessary touches outside B9.
annotation.bad_alternative_notes: assign_amenity clinic on B9 directly would be REJECTED ('block already has amenity fire'). redevelop with use=null on a non-protected/non-flood block would also be REJECTED. Building a fresh civic block elsewhere would waste land and infra.

source_to_constraint_mapping.planning_principles: High density, mixed land use, adequate street space, compact urban form.
source_to_constraint_mapping.inspired_civicflow_constraints: TOD concentrate-density constraint; mixed-use zoning allowed; district_targets.min_economic_ratio prevents residential monofunction.
source_to_constraint_mapping.status_labels: TOD constraint: SUPPORTED_NOW via zone choices and district_targets; economic_use_ratio: SUPPORTED_NOW via district_mix_gap_count + land_use_balance_score.

source_to_constraint_mapping.planning_principles: Universal proximity to green space (<=300 m); minimum per-capita green area.
source_to_constraint_mapping.inspired_civicflow_constraints: min_greenery_ratio hard target; greenery_shortfall metric; open_space zone and park use required for balance; per-district min_greenery_ratio in district_targets.
source_to_constraint_mapping.status_labels: min_greenery and greenery_ratio: SUPPORTED_NOW; per-district greenery floor: SUPPORTED_NOW; per-block proximity score for parks: SAFE_EXTENSION.

source_to_constraint_mapping.planning_principles: Walkability, accessible healthcare and daily services for ageing populations, adaptable infrastructure.
source_to_constraint_mapping.inspired_civicflow_constraints: district_service_gap_count enforces clinic / grocery / school coverage at the district level; replanning curveballs (fire -> clinic swap) test adaptability.
source_to_constraint_mapping.status_labels: Clinic and grocery amenities: SUPPORTED_NOW; district_service_gap_count + accessibility_score: SUPPORTED_NOW; per-block walking-radius coverage: SAFE_EXTENSION.

source_to_constraint_mapping.planning_principles: Maximise density within walking distance of high-capacity transit; prioritise non-motorised access; avoid auto-centric design.
source_to_constraint_mapping.inspired_civicflow_constraints: transit amenity siting via assign_amenity(transit, ...); 'tod_concentrate_density' constraint used in medium samples; per-block transit_access score is a safe extension.
source_to_constraint_mapping.status_labels: transit amenity: SUPPORTED_NOW; transit_access proximity score: SAFE_EXTENSION; TOD zoning pattern: SUPPORTED_NOW by example construction.

hackathon_scope_recommendation.tiny_medium_hard_mix: 60% tiny, 40% medium; drop hard from SFT (use it only as held-out evaluation).
recommended_safe_extensions_now: transit_access (per-block proximity score derived from assign_amenity(transit) placements)
per-block service_coverage proximity (graph traversal from residential blocks; complements district_service_gap_count)
now_unblocked_by_recent_env_changes: current_phase / phase_objective fields are already in the observation
district_service_gap_count, district_mix_gap_count, district_green_gap_count are already in last_metrics
land_use_balance_score and accessibility_score already feed reward components
defer_future_only: budget_remaining (no cost accounting in current verifier)
hackathon_scope_recommendation.notes: Hard trajectories are too complex to hand-author and often introduce noise; all SFT examples must pass deterministic verification. The corrected examples cover the critical skills: schema-correct formatting, phase-awareness, selective replanning under heritage/flood/capacity-cut curveballs, amenity co-location, and constraint adherence. Scaling to 100 step-examples is done by unrolling the 15 trajectories with the same fidelity and inserting intermediate observations between each turn.
